technique: Volumetric data exfiltration - one entity's outbound volume far above its baseline

trigger.from_breadth: An entity (SFTP Client_ip, IIS source IP / user on a data endpoint, or FortiGate srcip) whose outbound bytes in the window are far above normal, OR an entity already flagged by a compromise module (exfil right after a flagged login).

telemetry_required: SFTP: Size_In_MB + Client_ip (Cerberus source field is garbage - key on Client_ip)
IIS: Server_Bytes / sc_bytes + URI_Stream + Client_ip (raw message if parsed null)
telemetry_gap_behavior: If the byte field is null on the parsed event, sweep the raw message for the byte token before scoring; if no byte data exists for the channel, emit REVIEW volume-blind, never clean. SFTP source field is log-level tokens not a host - ALWAYS key SFTP on Client_ip.

method.goal: Sum the entity's outbound volume in the window, per channel
method.compute: SFTP: sum(Size_In_MB) by Client_ip ; IIS: sum(Server_Bytes/sc_bytes) by source on /download|/export|/api/document|/record endpoints ; Forti: sum(sentbyte) by srcip to external dstip
method.note: exact-count on the ONE entity - do not rely on a top-N aggregate that may truncate

method.goal: Establish the entity's baseline and score the deviation
method.compute: pull the entity's prior 7-day per-hour outbound volume -> mean + stdev -> z-score of the current window. Also test absolute floors.
method.decision_input: z-score > 3 above the entity's own mean, OR absolute: SFTP single Client_ip > 500 MB/window; IIS session sc_bytes > 100 MB on a data endpoint; Forti sentbyte to one external dst > 1 GB/window

method.compute: destination IP / country / ASN. External + new-country + hosting/VPS ASN raises severity; internal or known-backup target lowers it.
method.decision_input: large transfer to an external hosting/VPS/non-ACL destination = exfil; to a known internal backup/replication target = FP

method.check: is this entity ALSO flagged this run by credential-stuffing-pivot / impossible-travel / a Windows or Linux finding?
method.decision_input: volumetric egress from an entity with a concurrent compromise finding = post-compromise exfil -> escalate the whole chain to CRITICAL (killchain Actions)

decision.CRITICAL_conf5: above-baseline egress from an entity that ALSO has a compromise finding this run (login anomaly -> exfil chain)
decision.HIGH_conf4: z-score > 3 OR absolute floor breached, to an external / non-ACL / hosting destination, no benign explanation
decision.MEDIUM_conf3: large transfer but to a known/internal/Cato destination, or within a plausible-but-unconfirmed business pattern
decision.FP_kill: known scheduled backup / sync / replication job in the entity's baseline; Cato egress destination; transfer size within the entity's normal 7-day band

false_positive_checks: known backup / DB-dump / sync jobs that legitimately move large volumes on a schedule (confirm via baseline + destination)
Cato egress 140.82.202.196 / 199.27.40.187 / 123.253.153.138 as source or destination -> cap MEDIUM unless paired with a compromise finding
transfer size within the entity's own 7-day mean+/-3stdev = normal for this entity
internal replication / SAN / backup target as destination
